**Dynamic Kafka**

> This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use the latest [stable version](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/docs/connectors/table/dynamic-kafka/>).

#  Dynamic Kafka SQL Connector #

Scan Source: Unbounded

The Dynamic Kafka connector allows for reading data from Kafka topics that can move across clusters without restarting the job. Streams are resolved via a Kafka metadata service. This is especially useful for cluster migrations and dynamic topic/cluster changes.

##  Dependencies #

Maven dependency | SQL Client |


    &ltdependency&gt
          &ltgroupId&gtorg.apache.flink&lt/groupId&gt
          &ltartifactId&gtflink-connector-kafka&lt/artifactId&gt
          &ltversion&gt5.0.0-2.1&lt/version&gt
        &lt/dependency&gt

Copied to clipboard!  |  [Download](<https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/org/apache/flink/flink-sql-connector-kafka/5.0.0-2.1/flink-sql-connector-kafka-5.0.0-2.1.jar>)
---|---

The Kafka connector is not part of the binary distribution. See how to link with it for cluster execution [here](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.1/docs/dev/configuration/overview/>).

##  How to create a Dynamic Kafka table #

The example below shows how to create a Dynamic Kafka table using the built-in `single-cluster` metadata service. With this service, stream ids are interpreted as topics in a single Kafka cluster.


    CREATE TABLE DynamicKafkaTable (
      `user_id` BIGINT,
      `item_id` BIGINT,
      `behavior` STRING,
      `kafka_cluster` STRING METADATA FROM 'kafka_cluster',
      `ts` TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) METADATA FROM 'timestamp'
    ) WITH (
      'connector' = 'dynamic-kafka',
      'stream-ids' = 'user_behavior;user_behavior_v2',
      'metadata-service' = 'single-cluster',
      'metadata-service.cluster-id' = 'cluster-0',
      'properties.bootstrap.servers' = 'localhost:9092',
      'properties.group.id' = 'testGroup',
      'scan.startup.mode' = 'earliest-offset',
      'format' = 'csv'
    )


The connector also supports a custom metadata service via the `metadata-service` option. The service class must implement `KafkaMetadataService` and should either have a public no-arg constructor or a constructor that accepts `Properties`. The connector will pass the Kafka properties (all `properties.*` options) into the constructor when available.

##  Available Metadata #

The Dynamic Kafka connector exposes all metadata columns from the Kafka connector and adds one dynamic-source-specific metadata column:

  * `kafka_cluster` (`STRING NOT NULL`, read-only): cluster id resolved by the metadata service for the record.

See [Kafka SQL Connector](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.1/docs/connectors/table/kafka/#available-metadata>) for the shared metadata columns.

Example:


    CREATE TABLE DynamicKafkaTable (
      `user_id` BIGINT,
      `item_id` BIGINT,
      `behavior` STRING,
      `kafka_cluster` STRING METADATA FROM 'kafka_cluster' VIRTUAL
    ) WITH (
      'connector' = 'dynamic-kafka',
      'stream-ids' = 'user_behavior;user_behavior_v2',
      'metadata-service' = 'single-cluster',
      'metadata-service.cluster-id' = 'cluster-0',
      'properties.bootstrap.servers' = 'localhost:9092',
      'properties.group.id' = 'testGroup',
      'scan.startup.mode' = 'earliest-offset',
      'format' = 'csv'
    );


##  Connector Options #

Option | Required | Forwarded | Default | Type | Description
---|---|---|---|---|---

##### connector

| required | no | (none) | String | Specify what connector to use, for Dynamic Kafka use `'dynamic-kafka'`.

##### stream-ids

| optional | no | (none) | String | Semicolon-separated stream ids to subscribe. Only one of `stream-ids` and `stream-pattern` can be set.

##### stream-pattern

| optional | no | (none) | String | Regex pattern for stream ids to subscribe. Only one of `stream-ids` and `stream-pattern` can be set.

##### metadata-service

| required | no | (none) | String | Metadata service identifier. Use `'single-cluster'` or a fully qualified class name implementing `KafkaMetadataService`.

##### metadata-service.cluster-id

| optional | no | (none) | String | Cluster id required by `single-cluster` metadata service.

##### stream-metadata-discovery-interval-ms

| optional | no | -1 | Long | Interval in milliseconds for discovering stream metadata changes. A non-positive value disables discovery.

##### stream-metadata-discovery-failure-threshold

| optional | no | 1 | Integer | Number of consecutive discovery failures before failing the job.

The connector also supports the same format options and Kafka client properties as the Kafka connector. See [Kafka SQL Connector](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.1/docs/connectors/table/kafka/#connector-options>) for the full list of format options and Kafka properties (all `properties.*` options).
